info.title: Revel Digital REST API
info.description: Provides REST based access to your Revel Digital account

get.summary: Gets account details.

400.description: If the account API key is invalid
If the account API key is invalid

get.summary: Gets organizations.

400.description: If the account API key is invalid

get.summary: Gets audit events for the account.
get.description: Authorization: This endpoint requires the user to be an account owner.

parameters.name: httpMethod
parameters.description: Filter by HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Filter events after this date (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Filter events before this date (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (default: 100)

403.description: If the user is not an account owner

400.description: If the account API key is invalid

get.summary: Gets a single audit event by ID.
get.description: Authorization: This endpoint requires the user to be an account owner.

403.description: If the user is not an account owner

404.description: If the audit event is not found

400.description: If the account API key is invalid

post.summary:
Returns AdHawk dataset based on the provided post parameters.
This is a legacy endpoint that returns raw dataset results.
Consider using the newer typed endpoints for better structure.

get.summary: Gets play logs for the account.

parameters.description: Optional list of device IDs to filter by

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: Optional list of file IDs to filter by

parameters.description: Optional list of device IDs to filter by

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Time interval for aggregation (minute, hour, day, week, month)

parameters.name: groupByDevice
parameters.description: Group by device (default: false). When false, returns totals aggregated only by interval.

parameters.name: includeDwellTime

get.summary:
Gets event flow data for Sankey-type visualizations.
Builds a chronological sequence of events indicating the path users took through sessions.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Optional list of device IDs to filter by

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.description: Optional list of event names to filter by

parameters.name: eventDepthLevel
parameters.description: Maximum depth level for event flow (default: 6)

get.summary:
Gets geographic heatmap data aggregated by location.
Useful for heatmap or geographic visualizations.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Optional list of device IDs to filter by

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Time interval for aggregation (minute, hour, day, week, month)

get.summary:
Gets media impressions (OTS - Opportunity To See) data aggregated by media file.
Joins audience/impression data with play logs to determine which audience members
had an opportunity to see specific media content during playback.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Optional list of device IDs to filter by

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: Optional list of file/media IDs to filter by

parameters.name: minDwellThreshold
parameters.description: Minimum dwell time in milliseconds to count as "viewed" (default: 100ms)

get.summary:
Gets media impressions (OTS - Opportunity To See) data aggregated by device.
Joins audience/impression data with play logs to determine which devices
had the most audience engagement during media playback.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Optional list of device IDs to filter by

parameters.name: fileId
parameters.description: Optional list of file/media IDs to filter by

parameters.name: minDwellThreshold
parameters.description: Minimum dwell time in milliseconds to count as "viewed" (default: 100ms)

get.summary: Gets all alerts for the account.

parameters.name: active_only
parameters.description: Filter to show only active alerts (not resolved and not snoozed)

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to retrieve

get.summary: Gets an alert by ID.

patch.summary:
Partially updates an alert using JSON Patch.
Uses the JSON Patch syntax (http://jsonpatch.com)

get.summary: Lists data tables in the account.
Gets a data table definition by ID.

delete.summary: Deletes a data table and all its rows.

get.summary: Lists rows in a data table.

post.summary: Creates a new row in a data table.

get.summary: Gets a single row by ID.

post.summary: Batch creates rows in a data table.

put.summary: Reorders rows in a data table.

post.summary: Imports rows from a CSV or Excel (.xlsx) file.

get.summary: Exports rows as CSV.

post.summary: Deletes multiple rows in a single request. Max 100 row IDs per call.

get.summary: Lists version history for a row (newest first).
Gets a specific version snapshot of a row.

post.summary: Rolls back a row to a specific version.

get.summary: Gets all devices in an account.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to retrieve in a query.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Current page number for paginated results.

parameters.name: device_type_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the type of device.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: List of unique identifiers for media groups to filter by.

parameters.name: group_name
parameters.description: Collection of group names to filter by.

parameters.name: include_snap
parameters.description: Include the snapshot of the currently playing content. JPG format, base64 encoded.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for an organization to filter by.

parameters.name: is_online
parameters.description: Filter devices by their online status.

get.summary: Gets a device by ID.

Patch a device. Patch allows partial updates by targeting specific properties of the device.
Uses the JSON Patch syntax (http://jsonpatch.com)

parameters.name: take

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Optional number of page to return

Gets current snapshot of the device screen. Image is returned as a JPEG image.

parameters.name: base64
parameters.description: If true, the JPG image will be returned as a base64-encoded string

get.summary: Gets device ping data by registration key.

post.summary: Sends a collection of commands to devices.

get.summary: Returns a list of reports available for export.

schema.description: A collection of RevelDigital.Core.PublicApi.Models.Report items for XML serialization.

parameters.name: exclude_body
parameters.description:
Exclude the generated report body in the response.
            This report is always available at the URL provided in the X-RevelDigital-GeneratedReportUrl response header value.

Account.description:
Represents a customer account in the RevelDigital platform.
An account is the top-level entity that owns devices, media, playlists, templates, and schedules.

AdHawkAgeRange.description: Age range classification for audience analytics

period_start.description: Start of the time period for these metrics

period_end.description: End of the time period for these metrics

unique_visitors.description: Number of unique visitors (based on anonymous user IDs)

unknown_gender_count.description: Number of visitors with unknown gender

age_under_18.description: Number of visitors under 18 years old

age_over_74.description: Number of visitors over 74 years old

AdHawkAudienceMetrics.description: Aggregated audience metrics for a device over a time period

device_id.description: Array of devices by Id. REQUIRED

start_date.description:
Report start date. REQUIRED

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

end_date.description:
Report end date. REQUIRED

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

compare_start_time.description:
Report start time.

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

compare_end_time.description:
Report end time.

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

AdHawkDatasetPostBody.description: Represents the body of a request for an AdHawk dataset.

AdHawkDatasetType.description:
This enumeration defines the different categories of datasets that can be used for analytics
and reporting within the AdHawk platform. Each value corresponds to a specific type of data aggregation or
analysis, such as impressions, demographics, dwell times, events, or network-related metrics.

The following AdHawk dataset types are available:

ImpressionTotals: Total audience impressions for the given time period
GenderTotals: Audience impression totals grouped by gender
DailyImpressions: Total impressions grouped by interval (day, week, month, hour, minute)
DwellRange: Impressions grouped by the following dwell ranges in seconds: 0-5, 5-10, 10-15, 15-30, 30-45, 45-60, 60-120, 120+
AgeRange: Impressions grouped by the following age ranges: 0-17, 18-24, 25-34, 35-44, 45-54, 55-64, 65-74, 75+
DayOfWeekByHourHeatmap: Impressions grouped by day of week and hour of day
ImpressionsByGenderByDay: Impressions grouped by gender and day
ImpressionsByAgeGroupByDay: Impressions grouped by age group and day
GenderAgeHeatmap: Impressions grouped by gender and age
DailyAverageDwell: Average dwell time by day
ImpressionsByDeviceAndPingType: Impressions grouped by device and ping type
ImpressionsByMedia: Impressions grouped by media

EventsTotals: Total events and sessions for the given time period
DailyEvents: Total events grouped by interval (day, week, month, hour, minute)
EventsByEventName: Total events grouped by event name
EventsByDeviceName: Total events grouped by device name
EventsByDeviceCountry: Total events grouped by device country
AverageDwellByEventName: Average dwell time grouped by event name
EventsBySession: Total events grouped by session
AverageDwellBySession: Average dwell time grouped by session
AverageEventsByNameAndSession: Average events grouped by event name and session

NetworkPingTotals: Total device pings including percetage of time active, downloading, and playing. Grouped by interval (day, week, month, hour, minute)
NetworkAlertTotals: Totals alerts grouped by alert type and interval (day, week, month, hour, minute)
NetworkAlertsByLocation: Alerts grouped by location

total_impressions.description: Total number of impressions that occurred during media playback

unique_media_count.description: Number of unique media files played with audience present

net_reach.description: Net reach - count of impressions with dwell time > 100ms (actually viewed)

ots_percentage.description: OTS (Opportunity To See) percentage - ratio of net reach to total impressions

avg_dwell_time.description: Average dwell time in seconds for impressions with dwell > 100ms

unknown_gender_count.description: Count of impressions with unknown gender

avg_age.description: Average age of audience members (if available)

AdHawkDeviceMediaImpressions.description: Device-level media impression statistics showing OTS metrics per device.

period_start.description: Start of the time period for these metrics

period_end.description: End of the time period for these metrics

avg_cpu_usage.description: Average CPU usage percentage during the period

max_cpu_usage.description: Maximum CPU usage percentage during the period

avg_memory_usage.description: Average memory usage percentage during the period

max_memory_usage.description: Maximum memory usage percentage during the period

avg_disk_usage.description: Average disk usage percentage during the period

total_tx_bytes.description: Total bytes transmitted during the period

total_rx_bytes.description: Total bytes received during the period

uptime_percentage.description: Percentage of time the device was online (0-100)

ping_count.description: Number of pings received during the period

alert_count.description: Number of alerts generated during the period

AdHawkDeviceMetrics.description: Aggregated device health metrics over a time period

total_plays.description: Total number of media files played on this device

total_duration.description: Total duration of all plays in milliseconds

file_count.description: Number of unique media files played on this device

first_play.description: First play timestamp for the period

last_play.description: Last play timestamp for the period

AdHawkDevicePlayStats.description:
Device play statistics - aggregated play counts and duration for a device
Useful for DooH reporting to determine content playback by device

source.description: Source event name with index suffix (e.g., "Button Click1")

target.description: Target event name with index suffix (e.g., "Page View2")

value.description: Count of transitions from source to target

event_index.description: Position in the event sequence (1 = first transition, 2 = second, etc.)

avg_dwell.description: Average dwell time in seconds between source and target events

AdHawkEventFlow.description:
Event flow edge representing a transition between events in a user session.
Useful for building Sankey diagrams and analyzing user navigation paths.

id.description: Unique identifier for this event log entry

device_id.description: The device ID where the event occurred

session_id.description: Session identifier for grouping related events

properties.description: Additional properties associated with the event as key-value pairs

utc_offset.description: UTC offset of the device at the time of the event

AdHawkEventLog.description: Represents a custom event log entry from a device

event_name.description: The event name (when grouping by event)

period_start.description: Start of the time period for these metrics

period_end.description: End of the time period for these metrics

unique_event_names.description: Number of unique event names (when not grouping by event)

unique_devices.description: Number of unique devices (when not grouping by device)

AdHawkEventMetrics.description: Aggregated event metrics for a device over a time period

day.description: Day or period for the aggregated data

count.description: Total impression count for this location and period

AdHawkHeatMapData.description:
Geographic heatmap data point aggregated by location and time period.
Useful for geographic visualizations and location-based analytics.

id.description: Unique identifier for this impression entry

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the impression was recorded

device_id.description: The device ID where the impression was detected

type.description:
Type of impression detection.
Types are bitfield values:
<list type="bullet"><item><description>8 = motion</description></item><item><description>64 = audience</description></item><item><description>128 = ble</description></item><item><description>256 = wifi</description></item></list>

dwell_time.description: Dwell time in milliseconds - how long the visitor was detected

user_id.description: Anonymous user identifier for tracking unique visitors

rssi.description: Received Signal Strength Indicator for BLE/WiFi detections

utc_offset.description: UTC offset of the device at the time of detection

total_impressions.description: Total number of impressions that occurred during media playback

unique_device_count.description: Number of unique devices that played the media with audience present

net_reach.description: Net reach - count of impressions with dwell time > 100ms (actually viewed)

ots_percentage.description: OTS (Opportunity To See) percentage - ratio of net reach to total impressions

avg_dwell_time.description: Average dwell time in seconds for impressions with dwell > 100ms

unknown_gender_count.description: Count of impressions with unknown gender

avg_age.description: Average age of audience members (if available)

AdHawkMediaImpressions.description:
Media impression statistics showing OTS (Opportunity To See) metrics.
Joins audience/impression data with media play logs to determine which audience
members had an opportunity to see specific media content.

total_plays.description: Total number of times this media was played

total_duration.description: Total duration of all plays in milliseconds

device_count.description: Number of unique devices that played this media

AdHawkMediaPlayStats.description: Media play statistics - aggregated play counts for a media file

id.description: Unique identifier for this ping log entry

device_id.description: The device ID that sent the ping

type.description: Type of ping (alive, downloading, playing, motion)

sub_type.description: Subtype of the ping for more granular classification

tx_bytes.description: Total bytes transmitted since last ping

rx_bytes.description: Total bytes received since last ping

external_ip_address.description: External IP address of the device

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate if GPS is available

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate if GPS is available

AdHawkPingLog.description: Represents a device ping/heartbeat log entry - records device health and status

id.description: Unique identifier for this play log entry

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the content was played

device_id.description: The device ID where the content was played

file_id.description: The media file ID that was played

file_name.description: The name of the media file

duration.description: Duration of the play in milliseconds

utc_offset.description: UTC offset of the device at the time of play

AdHawkPlayLog.description: Represents a media play log entry - records when content was played on a device

device_id.description: Optional device ID (null if aggregated across all devices)

file_id.description: Optional file ID (null if aggregated across all files)

period_start.description: Start of the time period for these stats

period_end.description: End of the time period for these stats

total_plays.description: Total number of plays during this period

total_duration.description: Total duration of all plays in milliseconds

device_count.description: Number of unique devices (if aggregated by file)

file_count.description: Number of unique files (if aggregated by device)

AdHawkPlayStatsByTime.description:
Time-series play statistics aggregated by interval (hour, day, week, etc.)
Useful for DooH reporting to analyze playback trends over time

created_on.description: The date and time the alert was created

updated_on.description: The date and time the alert was last updated

snoozed_until.description: The date and time until which the alert is snoozed

resolved_on.description: The date and time the alert was resolved

devices.description: The devices affected by this alert

is_active.description: Indicates if the alert is currently active (not resolved and not snoozed)

snoozed_until.description: Set to snooze the alert until a specific date/time

AlertRequest.description: Alert model used for updating alerts

name.description: The name of the alert rule

is_enabled.description: Indicates if the alert rule is enabled

AlertRuleSummary.description: Alert rule summary (minimal info included with alerts)

timestamp.description: The date and time the event was recorded

event_type.description: The type of event (e.g., API call, login, etc.)

user_id.description: The user ID associated with the event (encrypted)

user_name.description: The name of the user associated with the event

account_id.description: The account ID associated with the event (encrypted)

ip_address.description: The IP address of the client

user_agent.description: The user agent string of the client

http_method.description: The HTTP method used (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)

controller_name.description: The name of the controller that handled the request

action_name.description: The name of the action that was executed

AuditEvent.description: Audit event model representing account activity

distance.description:
The detection distance threshold in meters.
Mobile devices within this range will receive beacon notifications.

calibration.description:
The RSSI calibration value (measured power at 1 meter) used for distance estimation.
This value is typically negative (e.g., -59 dBm) and device-specific.
Accurate calibration improves proximity zone detection accuracy.

phone.description:
Contact phone number associated with this beacon location.
Displayed to users who interact with the beacon via mobile app.

emailAddress.description:
Contact email address associated with this beacon location.
Displayed to users who interact with the beacon via mobile app.

website.description:
Website URL associated with this beacon location.
Can be opened by mobile app when user interacts with beacon notification.

devices.description:
List of device IDs associated with this beacon configuration.
Used for grouping multiple devices that share the same beacon identity.

rating.description:
Rating value for this beacon location (e.g., customer rating, priority score).
Can be used to rank or filter beacon-triggered content.

uuid.description:
The iBeacon UUID (Universally Unique Identifier).
A 128-bit identifier typically shared by all beacons in an organization.
Format: "XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX"

major.description:
The iBeacon Major value (0-65535).
Typically used to identify a specific location or venue within an organization.

minor.description:
The iBeacon Minor value (0-65535).
Typically used to identify a specific beacon within a location.

notification_delay.description:
Delay in seconds before sending a notification after beacon detection.
Prevents notification spam when users briefly pass by a beacon.
A value of 0 or null means immediate notification.

instance_id.description:
The Eddystone Instance ID (6 bytes, 12 hex characters).
Identifies a specific beacon within an Eddystone namespace.

namespace.description:
The Eddystone Namespace ID (10 bytes, 20 hex characters).
Typically derived from a domain name or organization identifier.

Beacon.description:
Represents Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) beacon configuration for a device.
Beacons enable proximity-based interactions, allowing mobile devices to detect nearby digital signage
and trigger location-aware content or notifications.

value_1.description: Value 1. See ConditionType documentation for usage notes.

value_2.description: Value 2. See ConditionType documentation for usage notes.

value_3.description: Value 3. See ConditionType documentation for usage notes.

value_4.description: Value 4. See ConditionType documentation for usage notes.

ConditionType.description:
The following enum represents the different types of conditions that can be used in scheduling logic.

Important usage notes specific to ConditionType:

- ConditionType: "TimeRange"
  Value1: "01/01/1970 12:00:00 AM" # Start time. Starts at this time. Date/time format: "MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss aa"
  Value2: "01/01/1970 12:00:00 AM" # End time. Stops at this time. Date/time format: "MM/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss aa"
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "TopicSet"
  Value1: "sports,news,weather" # Comma separted list of topics
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "TotalViewersGreaterThan"
  Value1: "50"
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "TotalViewersLessThan"
  Value1: "100"
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "TouchEvent"
  Value1: "tap"
  Value2: "screen1"
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "WeekOfMonth"
  Value1: "2"
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "WeekOfYear"
  Value1: "25"
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "GpsWithinRadius"
  Value1: "40.7128"
  Value2: "-74.0060"
  Value3: "50"
  Value4: "miles"
- ConditionType: "KeyEvent"
  Value1: "ENTER"
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "MonthOfYear"
  Value1: "12"
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "Motion"
  Value1: "detected"
  Value2: "5"
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "Never"
  Value1: null
  Value2: null
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "PctAdult"
  Value1: "60"
  Value2: ">="
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "PctChild"
  Value1: "25"
  Value2: "<="
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "PctFemale"
  Value1: "40"
  Value2: ">="
  Value3: null
  Value4: null
- ConditionType: "PctMale"
  Value1: "60"
  Value2: "<="
  Value3: null
  Value4: null

entered_service.description: The date the device was registered

language_code.description: The language code of the device

last_update.description: The timestamp of the last content sync with the server

mac_address.description: The device MAC address, if applicable

in_sync.description: Is the device content in sync with server

is_online.description:
Is the device currently online.
A device is considered online if it has a timestamp within the last 5 minutes

Device.description:
Represents a digital signage player device in the RevelDigital platform.
A device is a registered hardware unit that displays content according to assigned schedules and templates.

language_code.description: The language code of the device

mac_address.description: The MAC address of this device, is applicable

DeviceRequest.description:
Request model used for creating and updating devices in the RevelDigital platform.
Contains the mutable properties that can be set when registering or modifying a device.

id.description:
The unique identifier for the device type.
This is typically a string-based key used to reference the device type across the platform.

name.description:
The display name of the device type.
Examples include model names or product line identifiers (e.g., "BrightSign HD", "Samsung Tizen", "LG webOS").

manufacturer.description:
The manufacturer or vendor of the device type.
Examples include "BrightSign", "Samsung", "LG", "Android", etc.

device_caps.description:
A bitmask representing the capabilities supported by this device type.
Null indicates capabilities have not been defined for this device type.

DeviceType.description:
Represents a device type classification in the RevelDigital digital signage platform.
This DTO defines the hardware type and capabilities of a digital signage player device.
Device types are used to categorize devices by their manufacturer and supported features.

count.description: The number of items in this group

level.description: The nested level of this group

Group.description:
Represents an organizational folder/group for categorizing resources in the RevelDigital platform.
Groups form a hierarchical tree structure for organizing devices, media, playlists, templates, and schedules.

GroupRequest.description: Request model for creating a new group.

IntervalType.description:
Specifies the time interval granularity for aggregating analytics data.
Used with reporting and analytics endpoints to control data grouping.

LoopPolicyType.description:
Loop policy type determines how a source is to generatively positioned within a loop.
IMPORTANT: Playlist duration must be set for this to take effect. If no playlist duration is specified, then source loop policy should not be assigned.

tags.description: The tags associated with this media

is_shared.description: Is this media shared with other accounts

start_date.description: The start date for this media

end_date.description: The end date for this media

mime_type.description: The MIME type for this media

uploaded_on.description: The date the media was uploaded

uploaded_by_id.description: The user id that uploaded the media

sha_512.description: The SHA512 hash of the media file

Media.description:
Represents a media asset (image, video, audio, document) in the RevelDigital content library.
Media files are uploaded to the platform and referenced by playlists and templates for playback on devices.

tags.description: The tags associated with this media

is_shared.description: Is this media shared with other accounts

start_date.description: The start date for this media

end_date.description: The end date for this media

MediaRequest.description: Media request model. Used for creating and updating media

Module.description:
Represents a module (zone/region) within a template layout.
Modules define rectangular areas on screen where content from playlists is displayed.

users.description: The users assigned to this organization

Organization.description:
Represents an organization (reseller/partner) in the RevelDigital platform.
Organizations are parent entities that manage multiple RevelDigital.Core.PublicApi.Models.Accounts in multi-tenant scenarios.

mac_address.description: MAC address of primary player network interface

timestamp.description:
Timestamp of this ping.
Device is considered offline if this timestamp is older than 10 minutes

transfer_rate.description: Current transfer rate over the primary network interface

ip_address.description: IP address of the primary network interface

external_ip_address.description: IP address as seen by the server for this device

tags.description: The tags associated with this playlist

duration.description:
User-defined fixed duration for this playlist in seconds. Used in conjunction with loop policy
to control how sources are looped or truncated to fit within this fixed duration.
This is different from CalculatedDuration which represents the sum of all source durations.

calculated_duration.description:
The total calculated duration of the playlist in seconds, based on the sum of all source intervals.
This is a read-only property computed from individual source durations and cannot be updated via PUT/POST.

is_random_start.description: Randomize the order of the playlist

created_on.description: The date the playlist was created

created_by_id.description: The user id that created the playlist

modified_on.description: The date the playlist was last modified

modified_by_id.description: The user id that last modified the playlist

sources.description: The sources associated with this playlist

Playlist.description:
Represents a playlist containing an ordered collection of content sources for playback on digital signage devices.
Playlists define what content plays and in what order within a template module or schedule.

id.description:
The unique identifier for the report.
Corresponds to the SSRS report path/name.

name.description: The display name of the report shown in the UI.

description.description: A description of what the report contains and its purpose.

parameters.description:
The list of parameters required or optional for generating this report.
Parameters define filters such as date range, device selection, or grouping options.

Report.description:
Represents a report definition available in the RevelDigital reporting system.
Reports provide analytics and insights on device activity, content playback, and network health.

start_date.description:
Report start date.

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

end_date.description:
Report end date.

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

start_time.description:
Report start time.

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

end_time.description:
Report end time.

Provide in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ

device_id.description: Array of devices by Id to include in report

user_id.description: Array of users by Id to include in report

media_id.description: Array of media items by Id to include in report

ReportExportPostBody.description:
Represents the body of a request to export a report, including details such as the report identifier,  export
format, activity type, reporting interval, date and time ranges, and filters for devices, users,  and media
items.

id.description:
The parameter identifier used when submitting report requests.
This is the SSRS parameter name.

name.description: The display name/prompt shown to users when entering the parameter value.

required.description:
Indicates whether this parameter must be provided to generate the report.
True if the parameter is not nullable and does not allow blank values.

multi_value.description:
Indicates whether this parameter accepts multiple values.
When true, an array of values can be submitted for this parameter.

ReportParameter.description:
Represents a parameter definition for a report.
Parameters allow filtering and customizing report output (e.g., date range, device selection).

group_id.description: The group id to which the schedule belongs

tags.description: The tags associated with the schedule

monday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Monday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

tuesday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Tuesday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

wednesday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Wednesday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

thursday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Thursday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

friday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Friday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

saturday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Saturday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

sunday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Sunday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

start_date.description:
The start date for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

end_date.description:
The end date for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

start_time.description:
The start time for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

end_time.description:
The end time for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

template_id.description:
The template id for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

playlist_id.description:
The playlist id for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

devices.description: The devices associated with the schedule.

conditions.description:
The conditions associated with the schedule.
Only applicable for Campaign schedules.

group_name.description: The name of the group to which the schedule belongs

created_on.description: The date the schedule was created

created_by_id.description: The user id that created the schedule

modified_on.description: The date the schedule was last modified

modified_by_id.description: The user id that last modified the schedule

group_id.description: The group id to which the schedule belongs

tags.description: The tags associated with the schedule

monday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Monday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

tuesday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Tuesday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

wednesday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Wednesday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

thursday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Thursday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

friday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Friday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

saturday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Saturday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

sunday.description:
Enabled this schedule for playback on Sunday.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

start_date.description:
The start date for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

end_date.description:
The end date for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

start_time.description:
The start time for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

end_time.description:
The end time for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

template_id.description:
The template id for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

playlist_id.description:
The playlist id for the schedule.
Only applicable for Playlist and Template schedules.

devices.description: The devices associated with the schedule.

conditions.description:
The conditions associated with the schedule.
Only applicable for Campaign schedules.

ScheduleRequest.description:
Request model for creating and updating schedules in the RevelDigital platform.
Schedules define when and what content plays on devices by linking templates/playlists to time-based rules.

ScheduleType.description:
The following schedule types are available:

- Playlist
  Playlist is considered a basic schedule consisting of a single playlist with a date range, time range, and days of the week.
  Schedule properties to consider when using this type include the following: start_date, end_date, start_time, end_time, playlist_id, and monday-sunday
  
- Template
  Template is considered a basic schedule consisting of a single template with a date range, time range, and days of the week.
  Schedule properties to consider when using this type include the following: start_date, end_date, start_time, end_time, template_id, and monday-sunday
  
- Campaign
  Campaign schedules, or 'Smart Schedules', are more advanced consisting of multiple conditions logically grouped to
  determine what, when, and where content is played.
  Schedule properties to consider when using this type include an array of conditions.

sequence.description: The sequence. Lower numbers are displayed first.

Source.description:
Represents a content item within a playlist. A source defines what content to display
and for how long. Sources can reference media files, templates, embedded playlists, URLs, or other content types.

SourceType.description:
Specifies the content type of a RevelDigital.Core.PublicApi.Models.Source item within a playlist.
Each source type determines how the content is rendered and what properties are relevant.

orientation.description: Orientation (Landscape, Portrait, Landscape Reversed, Portrait Reversed)

Template.description:
Represents a screen layout template that defines the visual arrangement of content zones.
Templates specify where playlists are displayed on screen and are assigned to devices via schedules.

created_on.description: The date the user was created

role.description: The roles assigned to this user

last_activity.description: The date the user last logged in

email_alerts.description: Whether the user has email alerts enabled

sms_alerts.description: Whether the user has SMS alerts enabled

is_two_factor.description: Whether the user has two-factor authentication enabled

ApiKeyInHeader.description: Utilize a header based API key associated with your Revel Digital account. X-RevelDigital-ApiKey: XXXXXXXX
ApiKeyInHeader.name: X-RevelDigital-ApiKey

ApiKeyInQuery.description: Utilize a query based API key associated with your Revel Digital account. api_key=XXXXXXXXX
ApiKeyInQuery.name: api_key
